prompt: Why is Scotland part of England?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: Scotland is not part of England; rather, both Scotland and England are part of the United Kingdom. The United Kingdom (UK) is a sovereign country made up of four constituent countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland. Scotland and England were separate kingdoms until 1707, when the Acts of Union were passed, uniting them into a single kingdom known as Great Britain. This union was primarily political and economic, and Scotland has retained its own legal and educational systems.
